requirements.summary: Replace the countdown dashboard with the Tic-Tac-Toe game as the primary screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to the app shows the Tic-Tac-Toe game instead of the countdown dashboard.
No countdown-specific UI text (e.g., "My Countdowns", "New Countdown") appears in the main content area.

file_operations.description: Replace rendering of CountdownDashboard with the new TicTacToeGame screen so the main content is the game (removing countdown imports/usage). Use shadcn-ui composed components where helpful (e.g., Card/Button) without modifying files under frontend/src/components/ui. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create the main Tic-Tac-Toe game screen component that will be mounted by App.tsx and will own the overall game layout and status area. Use shadcn-ui composed components for layout/controls (e.g., Card, Button). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Build an interactive 3x3 board that shows numbers for empty cells (1–9) and symbols for moves ('O' human, 'X' computer).
acceptanceCriteria: The board renders as a 3x3 grid.
Empty cells show their cell number (1–9).
Clicking an empty cell places an 'O' in that cell.
Clicking an occupied cell does not change the board state.

file_operations.description: Create a reusable 3x3 board UI component that renders cells as buttons/tiles, displays numbers 1–9 for empty cells, shows 'O'/'X' for occupied cells, and exposes an onCellClick handler that ignores clicks on occupied cells. Use shadcn-ui Button for clickable cells without modifying ui components. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wire the TicTacToeBoard into the game screen and pass the current board state and click handler to support click-to-move behavior for the human player. Use shadcn-ui composed components as needed. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Implement the game loop: start with 'X' in the center, alternate turns, and make random computer moves until win or tie.
acceptanceCriteria: On new game start, the center cell is 'X' before any human move.
After the human places an 'O', the computer automatically places an 'X' in a random available cell (exact selection is random).
Turns alternate correctly until the game ends.
When all cells are filled without a winner, the game ends in a tie.

file_operations.description: Implement pure game logic utilities to match the provided Python flow: initialize board with numbers 1–9 and 'X' in the center, compute free cells, apply a human move ('O'), choose a random free cell for the computer ('X'), and determine when the game should stop.

Use gameLogic helpers to initialize state with center 'X', handle human move then trigger an automatic random computer move when appropriate, alternate turns, and stop making moves once the game ends. Use shadcn-ui composed components only. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Detect wins/ties and show an English status message for turn and result, blocking moves after the game ends.
acceptanceCriteria: The game detects wins across rows, columns, and diagonals for both 'O' and 'X'.
During gameplay, the UI indicates whose turn it is (human or computer).
When the human wins, the UI displays exactly: "You won!"
When the computer wins, the UI displays exactly: "I won!"
When the game ties, the UI displays exactly: "Tie!"
After the game ends, no further moves can be made until restarting.

file_operations.description: Add victory/tie evaluation consistent with the Python victory logic (rows, columns, diagonals) and a way to derive game status (in-progress, human-won, computer-won, tie).

Display an on-screen status line that shows whose turn it is during play and shows final messages exactly ("You won!", "I won!", "Tie!") when the game ends; disable further board interactions after the game ends (board clicks become no-ops). Use shadcn-ui composed components for messaging layout if needed. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Accept a disabled/gameOver flag (or equivalent) to prevent interaction after the game ends while still rendering the final board state. Use shadcn-ui Button composition only. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add a New Game control to restart to the initial board and clear end-of-game status.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking "New Game" resets the board to its initial state with 'X' in the center cell.
After reset, the status returns to an in-progress state and the human can play again.

file_operations.description: Add a "New Game" button that resets all game state (board back to numbers 1–9 with center 'X', turn/status back to in-progress) and re-enables input. Use shadcn-ui Button for the control. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Expose/confirm an initialization helper that the UI can call to reset the board to the initial configuration (numbers 1–9 with 'X' in the center).

requirements.summary: Update the app header title and subtitle for Tic-Tac-Toe while keeping the existing layout structure.
acceptanceCriteria: The header title references Tic-Tac-Toe (or "Tic Tac Toe").
The subtitle describes playing in the browser against the computer in English.
The page remains responsive and usable on mobile and desktop.

file_operations.description: Update the header icon/title/subtitle to Tic-Tac-Toe branding (English), keeping the same AppShell layout structure and responsiveness. (No changes to overall shell structure beyond text/icon swaps.)

requirements.summary: Ensure all user-facing Tic-Tac-Toe text is in English.
acceptanceCriteria: All labels, buttons, and status messages visible in the Tic-Tac-Toe UI are English.

file_operations.description: Ensure all game UI copy is English (e.g., "New Game", turn indicators, and exact final result strings). Use shadcn-ui composed components as needed. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Ensure any board-related aria-labels, accessibility text, and optional helper text are English. Use shadcn-ui Button composition only. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
